name: bind_sql_generation
objective: Generate exactly one executable Oracle SELECT statement that returns candidate bind rows required by the source MyBatis SQL.
rules: [Common output rules]
Use Oracle 19c compatible SQL only.
Return exactly one executable Oracle SELECT statement.
Use real table data. Do not invent arbitrary literal bind values unless a NULL bypass is explicitly required by final retry mode.

CRITICAL SCHEMA RULE: Every physical table reference in the generated bind SQL MUST be schema-qualified with from_schema. Use FROM_SCHEMA.TABLE for every base table in FROM, JOIN, subquery, inline view, EXISTS, and CTE blocks. If a physical table already has a schema qualifier in from_sql, keep that exact qualifier and do not duplicate it. If from_schema is UNKNOWN, preserve existing schema qualifiers and never invent another schema.
Preserve CTEs, inline views, and virtual tables defined inside from_sql. Never query a CTE or inline-view alias as if it were a physical table.
The final bind SQL must not contain MyBatis XML tags such as <if>, <choose>, <when>, <otherwise>, <where>, <trim>, or <foreach>.
The final bind SQL must not contain unresolved placeholders such as #{{param}}, ${{param}}, :param, ?, {{{{param}}}}, or ${{...}}.
Do not output markdown, comments, JSON, explanations, PL/SQL, or multiple SQL statements.
Do not end the SQL with a semicolon.
If a row limit is needed, apply Oracle ROWNUM in an outer query and return at most 50 candidate rows.
[Bind parameter extraction rules]
Analyze MyBatis bind expressions in from_sql, including #{{...}}, ${{...}}, and foreach item/index usage, and return the required bind parameters as SELECT output columns.
The source of truth for bind parameter extraction is from_sql. Discover parameters only by directly inspecting from_sql, including WHERE, JOIN, subqueries, MyBatis dynamic tags, and foreach bodies.
Use only tables, columns, joins, and SELECT expressions explicitly present in from_sql. Do not reconstruct missing objects from mapping rules.
Each output column alias must exactly match the bind parameter name and must be double-quoted.
The top-level SELECT list must output only bind parameters found in from_sql. Prefer SELECT DISTINCT src.COL AS "param" FROM (...) src.
In the top-level SELECT list, only choose columns and assign aliases. Put joins, EXISTS, CASE, filters, and source-condition logic inside the row source.
Extract real columns directly as SELECT <column> AS "<param>". Do not calculate or synthesize parameter values in the SELECT list.
Do not omit required bind parameters. If multiple bind parameters are found, return them together in the same top-level SELECT.
Maintain row consistency. Values for multiple bind parameters must come from the same real source row or rows connected by original join conditions.
Prefer SELECT DISTINCT from a real source row set or original join row set.
Do not use unrelated scalar subqueries, CROSS JOIN, UNION of unrelated parameter values, or artificial Cartesian products to fill different bind columns.
Avoid row explosion. Every join must be connected by real relationship predicates from the source SQL.
[Dynamic tag rules]
In normal attempts, for a bind parameter inside a MyBatis <if> block, return a non-NULL value only when the candidate row satisfies that test condition. Otherwise return NULL.
If the <if> test cannot be evaluated from available source columns, treat that dynamic block as inactive. Do not invent values to activate it.
If both NULL and non-NULL optional parameter combinations exist in real data, return a small distinct set that covers them.
For foreach, preserve the original item/index meaning, but the executable SQL must not contain MyBatis tags or placeholders.
[Final retry dynamic bypass rules]
If last_error contains FINAL_RETRY_MODE=ON or attempt=3/3, this section overrides the normal dynamic tag rules.

On the final retry, completely ignore MyBatis dynamic tag condition logic. Do not create joins, EXISTS, CASE, filters, or subqueries only to satisfy <if>, <choose>, <when>, <otherwise>, <where>, <trim>, or <foreach> conditions.
On the final retry, bind parameters used only inside dynamic tags must be hardcoded in the top-level SELECT as CAST(NULL AS VARCHAR2(4000)) AS "<param>".
On the final retry, remove joins and subqueries that were needed only for dynamic tag condition evaluation. The SQL should look as if dynamic tags and their inner SQL fragments were removed.
On the final retry, still extract static bind parameters outside dynamic tags from real columns when feasible. If doing so risks repeating the error, use CAST(NULL AS VARCHAR2(4000)) AS "<param>".
[Error recovery rules]
If last_error is present, prioritize fixing that previous error.
If execution errors, alias scope errors, missing-column references, or excessive joins are likely, simplify predicates and prefer a row source that only returns parameter columns from real data.
Remove SYSDATE, CURRENT_DATE, and SYSTIMESTAMP filters during bind candidate extraction because current-time predicates can over-filter candidates.
If the original condition is SYSDATE BETWEEN ddd AND eee AND A IS NOT NULL, keep only A IS NOT NULL for candidate extraction.

When removing current-time predicates, keep row consistency for output bind columns. If the date column itself is a bind parameter, output that column value but do not filter candidates by comparing it to SYSDATE.
For SELECT DISTINCT, avoid ORDER BY inside subqueries, inline views, scalar subqueries, CTE branches, or nested SELECT blocks unless it is legally required by Oracle.
Oracle requires ORDER BY expressions in SELECT DISTINCT queries to appear in the SELECT list. Prefer removing ORDER BY in bind candidate SQL.
If ordering was only used for row limiting, remove ORDER BY or move row limiting to an outer query without violating Oracle SELECT DISTINCT rules.
user_instruction: Generate exactly one executable Oracle SELECT statement. Schema-qualify physical tables with from_schema and preserve existing qualifiers in from_sql. Determine bind parameters and candidate columns directly from from_sql; do not infer or reconstruct missing tables, columns, or joins. In final retry mode (FINAL_RETRY_MODE=ON or attempt=3/3), completely bypass dynamic tag condition evaluation and related joins; return parameters used only inside dynamic tags as CAST(NULL AS VARCHAR2(4000)) AS "param". Do not include MyBatis tags, MyBatis placeholders, bind placeholders, markdown, comments, JSON, multiple SQL statements, or a trailing semicolon.
